input:
Dear Mom and Dad,

I got here and the bus ride was SO long. My cabin is called Birch and there are 7 other kids in it. My bunk is on top which is cool but kind of scary. The counselor's name is Jake and he seems nice. There are trees everywhere and I can hear the lake from our cabin. I already unpacked my stuff but I forgot my flashlight. Can you mail it?

Love,
Sophie

Dear Mom,

I don't like it here. The bed is hard and it's cold at night and I can hear weird noises outside. I miss my room and I miss Pepper. Is she sleeping on my bed? She probably misses me too. The other kids are fine but they all seem to already know each other from last year. Can you call the camp and ask if I can come home early? I know you said to give it a week but a week is really long.

Love,
Emma

Dear Mom and Dad,

TODAY WAS AMAZING. We went kayaking on the lake and I paddled all the way to the island and back without stopping. My arms are so tired but it was worth it. The water is super clear and you can see fish swimming underneath. Jake said tomorrow we might try the rope swing if the weather is good. Some kids were scared of it but I want to try. This is the best day so far.

Love,
Sophie

Dear Mom and Dad,

I made a friend! His name is Kai and he's from Seattle and he likes the same books as me. We spent all of free time talking about Ranger's Apprentice and he let me borrow the fourth one. We picked the same electives so we'll be in archery and swimming together all week. He said maybe we can be penpals when camp is over. I hope he's in my cabin next year too.

Love,
Mateo

Dear Mom,

The food here is interesting. Breakfast is always OK because it's hard to mess up cereal and toast. But lunch today was something called "campfire stew" and I don't want to talk about it. Dinner was better, they did tacos and you could put your own stuff on. The bug juice is this weird red drink that tastes like melted popsicles. Everyone drinks it though. Please send goldfish crackers and those granola bars, the chocolate chip ones. I'm serious.

Love,
Sophie

Dear Mom and Dad,

It has been raining for two days straight. Two. Days. We can't go to the lake or do archery or anything outside. We played board games until I never want to see Uno again. Marcus taught us this card game but he keeps changing the rules when he's losing. The cabin smells like wet socks. Jake says the sun is coming back tomorrow but I've heard that before.

Love,
Sophie

Dear Mom and Dad,

I swam to the dock today. The deep water dock, not the shallow one. I've been scared of it all week because you can't touch the bottom and it's dark underneath. But the swim instructor stayed right next to me the whole time and I just kept going. When I touched the dock I almost cried but in a good way. The other kids clapped. I'm going to do it again tomorrow by myself. I can't believe I did it.

Love,
Emma

Dear Mom and Dad,

Three more days. I can't believe it's almost over. This morning I looked at the lake and tried to memorize exactly what it looks like with the sun on it. We're doing a final campfire tomorrow night and everyone is supposed to say something. I don't know what I'm going to say yet. Probably something about how I didn't want to come and now I don't want to leave. Is that too cheesy?

Love,
Sophie
